title: penstemon heterophyllus subsp. australis
intro: Penstemon heterophyllus subsp. australis is a subspecies of the Penstemon heterophyllus plant, commonly known as the Foothill Penstemon, which is a flowering plant native to the southwestern region of North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of coughs, colds, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Penstemon heterophyllus subsp. australis include its ability to soothe sore throats and coughs, reduce inflammation, and act as an expectorant to help clear mucus from the lungs. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
